sections.paragraph:
ἀνωτερικός, ή, όν (s. ἀνώτερος; Hippocr.; Galen [Hobart 148]; Hippiatr. I 69, 22) upper τὰ ἀ. μέρη the upper (i.e. inland ) country, the interior Ac 19:1 (like ἄνω for the interior, Jdth 1:8; 2:21 al. LXX).

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Galen Galen , II a.d.—List 5

Hobart Hobart = WH., Medical Language of St. Luke—List 6

Hippiatr. Hippiatr. = Corpus hippiatricorum Graecorum, IX a.d.—List 5

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 92
